word_list.word: [let] the cat out of the bag
word_list.pronunciation: lˈɛt ðə kˈæt ˌaʊɾəv ðə bˈæɡ

senses.definition: to reveal information that were supposed to be kept secret, often by accident
to reveal a secret that one was asked not to tell anyone

examples.x: Sarah was planning a surprise party for her friend, but she accidentally spilled the beans when she mentioned it in front of him.
The detective interrogated the suspect, hoping they would spill the beans about their involvement in the crime.
John couldn't keep a secret and always ended up spilling the beans about surprises his friends had planned for others.
The journalist promised to protect their source's identity but ended up spilling the beans during a live interview.
The company's CEO accidentally spilled the beans about their upcoming merger during a casual conversation, causing a frenzy in the business world.

senses.definition: used of a secret that is now shared with others

examples.x: The scandal was finally in the open after a whistleblower came forward.
Sarah's relationship with her co-worker was a secret until they were spotted holding hands in the office cafeteria, making their romance in the open.
The government promised to bring corruption cases out in the open, ensuring transparency and accountability in public affairs.
After years of speculation, the celebrity couple confirmed their engagement and made their wedding plans in the open for all their fans to see and celebrate.
The scandal that had been kept hidden for years was finally out in the open after a whistleblower came forward.

senses.definition: to reveal a person's identity or intentions

examples.x: The journalist accidentally revealed the source's identity, unknowingly blowing their cover and undermining their safety.
The agent had to escape the enemy's grasp after a security breach blew their cover, leaving them exposed in hostile territory.

senses.definition: to see or arrest a person while they are doing something illegal or wrong
to arrest or see someone the moment they are doing something that is illegal or dishonest
to publicly talk about something such as a belief that one used to keep secret

examples.x: She comes out of the closet and shares her true beliefs with her friends.
He is coming out of the closet today to openly express his political views.
They come out of the closet and reveal their long-held secret to the world.
She came out of the closet last year and received overwhelming support from her family.
He came out of the closet in high school and faced some initial challenges but ultimately found acceptance.
By next month, she will come out of the closet and openly discuss her spiritual beliefs.
She is currently coming out of the closet and bravely voicing her opinions.

senses.definition: to reveal something that one was supposed to keep as a secret or surprise

examples.x: The chess player gave the game away by making a careless move, allowing their opponent to capture their queen.
The team's quarterback inadvertently gave the game away by revealing their offensive strategy during a press conference.
The spy accidentally gave the game away by mentioning classified information in a public conversation, jeopardizing the mission.
The poker player's nervous reaction gave the game away, indicating that they had a weak hand.
The contestant gave the game away by answering a question incorrectly during the final round of the quiz show.

senses.definition: to cause the truth become revealed, especially an unpleasant one

examples.x: The investigative journalist's report lifted the lid on the corruption scandal, revealing the extent of the bribery and fraud.
The whistleblower's testimony provided crucial evidence, lifting the lid on the company's unethical practices.
The documentary film aims to lift the lid on the environmental impact of the fashion industry, highlighting its harmful practices.
The memoir by the former insider lifted the lid on the inner workings of the political campaign, exposing hidden agendas and strategies.
The leaked documents lifted the lid on the organization's discriminatory practices, sparking public outrage and demands for accountability.

senses.definition: to tell others about what has happened to someone, especially about things that are private or controversial, in the person's absence

examples.x: Sarah is known for spilling the tea about her friends' love lives, sharing all the juicy details and scandals.
During the party, John couldn't resist spilling the tea about the secret romance between two of their colleagues.
The reality TV show specializes in contestants spilling the tea on their fellow competitors, creating drama and suspense.
The gossip column in the magazine always spills the tea on the latest celebrity scandals and controversies.
In the group chat, everyone was eager to spill the tea about the recent workplace drama, sharing rumors and speculations.
After years of denial, he finally saw the light of day and admitted his mistakes publicly.
The company's unethical practices were exposed, and the executives had no choice but to see the light and take responsibility for their actions.
It took some time, but eventually, she saw the light and recognized the toxic nature of her relationship.
The politician initially opposed the policy, but when presented with compelling evidence, he saw the light of day and changed his stance.
The whistleblower's courageous act helped the public see the light about the corruption happening within the organization.

senses.definition: to challenge a person to carry out their seemingly fake threats or claims in order to expose them

examples.x: She claimed she could beat anyone in a race, so I called her bluff and challenged her to a sprint.
